troparia: (Tone 6) Standing before the cross with the virgin disciple,* the Virgin Mother could not endure* to behold her Offspring being lifted on the cross.* Overwhelmed with sorrow she cried out:* How can You suffer, O You Who by nature cannot suffer?* O my Son, I glorify Your ineffable goodness* which surpasses all comprehension.
kontakia: (Tone 8) Seeing You nailed on the cross, O Jesus,* accepting the passion freely, O Master,* Your virgin Mother cried out:* Woe is me, O sweet Child* How unjust the wounds You suffer,* O Healer of human frailties,* Who deliver from corruption through Your mercy.
